title: Unsolved-Relay Triage Row
description: Schema for one row in targets/triage.json, per Mission Brief §M1.1.

problem.description: Common name of the unsolved problem (e.g., 'Erdős–Straus conjecture').

category.description: Top-level subject area. Survey spans at least 5 distinct categories per §M1.1.

known_partial_results_url.description: URL (Wikipedia / arXiv / DOI / OEIS) anchoring the strongest known partial result. Empty string allowed only if literature is too sparse to cite a single canonical anchor — flag in falsifiable_sub_question.

machinery_required.description: List of techniques needed for the bounded sub-question (e.g., 'PSLQ at 200 dps', 'Lean 4 Mathlib4', 'sieve-of-Atkin').

machinery_available_locally.description: TRUE iff every item in machinery_required has been previously deployed by the operator (per Mission Brief §M1.1 hard filter). FALSE → row must NOT proceed to M1.2 selection.

falsifiable_sub_question.description: Single bounded sub-question that M2–M6 will assault. Must be falsifiable within 5 weeks on a single workstation.

estimated_compute_to_partial_result_hour.description: Honest upper-estimate of CPU-hours to reach the partial/negative result the success predicate would accept. Cap is 5 weeks × 168 h/week = 840 h.

publishability_of_negative_result.description: How venue-receptive the community is to a documented negative or bounded-range result on this problem.

AEAL_compliance_risk.description: Risk that the bounded sub-question will invite Searcher's-Fatigue substitution (sweep-for-reasoning). HIGH = avoid; LOW = clean AEAL fit.
